notes: Without skill, model honestly admits no knowledge of itwillsync and falls back to generic subcommand guesses
Skill delivers 100% pass rate vs 25% baseline — a 75-point improvement across all 3 evals
Without skill, model gives technically reasonable but wrong-tool answers (tmux+ttyd, aider --browser) on evals 1 and 2
Without skill, eval 3 is the most dramatic failure: model honestly admits no itwillsync knowledge and falls back to generic guesses
Skill runs are FASTER (avg 18.9s vs 32.9s) — model with skill goes direct to the right answer with less exploration
The 'no-hallucinated-tool' and 'correct-command' assertions are the most discriminating — all fail without skill, all pass with skill
The 'no-restart-advice' and 'no-app-install' assertions pass in both configs — low discriminating value, consider dropping or replacing with stronger variants
